Title: Metatranscriptomics reveals a differential temperature effect on the structural and functional organization of the anaerobic food web in rice field soil

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Undetectable at 30 °C; rapidly increased from <0.5% on day 5 to 46.9% on day 16 at 45 °C and remained high thereafter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential ecological indicator ecotype for warming-driven shifts in methane-producing communities in rice paddies.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Thermophilic enrichment at 45 °C during later incubation stage
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A new Methanocella ecotype proliferated under thermophilic conditions and dominated hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a climatically important greenhouse gas.
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Methanocellaceae population in rice field soil
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Methanocella_3 was absent at 30 °C but strongly enriched at 45 °C. 2. Its dynamics closely matched mcrA transcript:gene ratios and hydrogenotrophic methanogenic activity. 3. The population may represent a novel ecotype with a temperature optimum around 45 °C.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a phylogenetic population/ecotype rather than a canonical named gene; included because it is a primary experimentally resolved core functional unit in the Results.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metatranscriptomics (PRJNA412498); EMIRGE assembly of 16S rRNA sequences; assembled sequences accession MG977525-MG977676
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMIRGE reconstruction of near full-length 16S rRNA sequences; temporal abundance profiling; association with mcrA transcript dynamics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil with rice straw at 45 °C

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Prevalent at 30 °C; steadily decreased from 50.8% on day 5 to low level by day 30; not prevalent at 45 °C
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Ecological indicator of mesophilic hydrogenotrophic methanogen population structure in rice paddies.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Methanocella_1 represented a mesophilic Methanocellaceae population that declined over time and was disfavored at 45 °C.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a climatically important greenhouse gas.
Common_Genes.Source_Species: Methanocellaceae population in rice field soil
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Methanocella_1 was prevalent under mesophilic conditions. 2. It declined over incubation and was not favored at thermophilic temperature. 3. The data suggest phylogeny-linked temperature preference within Methanocellaceae.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Population/ecotype rather than a canonical gene.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Metatranscriptomics (PRJNA412498); EMIRGE-assembled 16S rRNA sequences accession MG977525-MG977676
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMIRGE reconstruction and temporal abundance profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil with rice straw at 30 °C

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Wood-Ljungdahl pathway / reductive acetogenesis; syntrophic acetate oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fhs transcript abundance was significantly higher at 45 °C than at 30 °C. 2. The temporal dynamics of fhs expression matched acetate turnover and Gibbs free energy calculations. 3. The data support fhs as a marker of syntrophic acetate oxidation at 45 °C, but not at 30 °C. 4. Thermoanaerobacteraceae, and likely Heliobacteriaceae and OPB54-related Firmicutes, contributed to the fhs transcript pool under thermophilic conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metatranscriptomic quantification of fhs transcripts; taxonomic assignment of fhs mRNA reads; correlation with acetate concentration and Gibbs free energy profiles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a major greenhouse gas generated through anaerobic carbon mineralization in flooded soils.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher fhs expression at 45 °C indicates enhanced syntrophic acetate oxidation feeding hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Acetate oxidation branch linked to syntrophic acetate oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil slurry amended with rice straw; incubation at 30 °C versus 45 °C over 30 days
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Rice field soil slurry headspace/porewater system
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct crop-breeding value; useful as a biomarker to monitor temperature-driven shifts toward syntrophic acetate oxidation and methane formation in paddy soils.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes that fhs is not uniquely diagnostic for acetate oxidation under all conditions, because it also functions in reductive acetogenesis; interpretation depends on thermodynamics.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript abundance peaked most strongly around day 23 at 45 °C; low at 30 °C
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent conversion between formate plus tetrahydrofolate and 10-formyltetrahydrofolate in the C1 transfer pathway; interpreted here as associated with syntrophic acetate oxidation under thermophilic conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mcrA transcript dynamics tracked methanogenic activity at both temperatures. 2. At 30 °C, mcrA dynamics reflected early acetoclastic Methanosarcinaceae activity and later increased contribution of Methanocellaceae. 3. At 45 °C, mcrA transcripts were dominated by Methanocellaceae and corresponded closely to proliferation of Methanocella_3. 4. qPCR/RT-qPCR mcrA transcript:gene ratios independently supported shifts in methanogenic activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metatranscriptomic analysis of mcrA transcripts; qPCR and RT-qPCR of mcrA genes and transcripts; taxonomic assignment of methanogen transcripts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a climatically important greenhouse gas produced in anaerobic food webs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mcrA expression marked temperature-dependent shifts between acetoclastic and hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, with strong hydrogenotrophic dominance at 45 °C.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Methanogenic archaea in rice field soil microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metatranscriptomics (NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA412498; SRR6122723-SRR6122752); qPCR/RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil slurry amended with rice straw; incubation at 30 °C versus 45 °C over 30 days
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful environmental biomarker for monitoring methanogenic pathway shifts under warming scenarios in rice paddies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper used mcrA primarily as an activity marker of methanogenic guilds rather than to test a single isolate gene function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At 30 °C, highest in early stage with Methanosarcinaceae and later shifted toward Methanocellaceae; at 45 °C, strongly associated with Methanocellaceae and greatest later-stage activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the final methane-forming reduction of methyl-coenzyme M with coenzyme B to methane and CoM-S-S-CoB.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACDS transcripts were characteristic of acetoclastic methanogenesis. 2. Their dynamics at 30 °C paralleled Methanosarcinaceae activity during the early incubation stage. 3. The data supported dominant acetoclastic methanogenesis early under mesophilic conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metatranscriptomic profiling of methanogen functional transcripts with temporal comparison to archaeal rRNA, total mRNA and methane production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a greenhouse gas end product of anaerobic carbon turnover.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated ACDS transcript abundance at 30 °C indicated active acetoclastic methanogenesis, especially early in incubation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Acetyl-CoA / acetate-derived carbon in acetoclastic methanogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Methanosarcinaceae-dominated methanogens in rice field soil microbiome
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required in the acetoclastic route leading to methane
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil slurry amended with rice straw; incubation at 30 °C versus 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker of acetoclastic methanogenesis in paddy soil ecology rather than a direct crop engineering target.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported as enzyme complex transcripts characteristic of acetoclastic methanogenesis; no single gene accession was provided.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher during early mesophilic incubation, concomitant with Methanosarcinaceae dominance
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetyl-CoA decarbonylase/synthase complex catalyzes cleavage/synthesis reactions in the acetoclastic methanogenesis pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mer transcripts were characteristic of hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis. 2. Their abundance pattern, together with frh, supported increasing hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, especially at 45 °C. 3. The transcript pool was associated mainly with Methanocellaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metatranscriptomic profiling of methanogenic pathway transcripts and taxonomic linkage to methanogen families
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a greenhouse gas generated in anaerobic soil systems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative mer-associated activity indicated strong hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis under thermophilic conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Methanocellaceae-dominated methanogens in rice field soil microbiome
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required in pathway leading to methane from H2/CO2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil slurry amended with rice straw; incubation at 30 °C versus 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Environmental functional marker for hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis under warming, not a direct crop trait target.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as pathway-indicative transcript rather than as a single experimentally isolated gene.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: More prominent in later mesophilic stage and strongly dominant at 45 °C

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. frh transcripts, together with mer, were indicative of hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis. 2. The transcript pattern supported dominance of Methanocellaceae-mediated hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, especially at 45 °C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metatranscriptomic profiling of methanogenic pathway transcripts and taxonomic assignment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Methane is a greenhouse gas generated by anaerobic microbial food webs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher frh-associated activity reflected increased hydrogenotrophic methane formation under thermophilic conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Methanocellaceae-dominated methanogens in rice field soil microbiome
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies reducing equivalents for methane formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Anoxic rice field soil slurry amended with rice straw; incubation at 30 °C versus 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Environmental activity marker for methanogenic pathway analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as part of enzyme complex signatures of hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis, not as an individually isolated locus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly represented in methanogen mRNA pool at 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes H2 and transfers electrons to coenzyme F420 in hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cellulase transcripts were among the key CAZyme signals linked to rice straw degradation. 2. At 45 °C, cellulase expression peaked around day 16 and was dominated by Firmicutes, especially Clostridiaceae and Ruminococcaceae. 3. At 30 °C, cellulase-related transcripts arose from more taxonomically diverse populations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CAZyme-focused metatranscriptomics using dbCAN annotation and taxonomic assignment of CAZyme mRNA reads
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polymer breakdown supplies fermentable substrates that ultimately fuel acetate and methane production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced cellulase expression under thermophilic conditions accelerated early-stage polymer hydrolysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fermentation and methanogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rice straw amendment; incubation at 30 °C or 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Polysaccharide-derived carbon flux supporting methane formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for residue management strategies affecting methane emission rather than direct plant gene engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats cellulase as a functional CAZyme class, not a single gene locus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At 45 °C peaked around day 16; at 30 °C persisted more gradually over incubation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Polymer hydrolysis was inferred to become rate-limiting during later mesophilic incubation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes β-1,4-glucan bonds in cellulose to release soluble sugars.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Endo-1,4-beta-xylanase transcripts were key markers of rice straw hemicellulose breakdown. 2. At 45 °C, expression was dominated by Firmicutes and showed dynamics similar to cellulase, peaking around day 16. 3. The data suggest highly interrelated cellulose and xylan degradation during thermophilic polymer breakdown.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CAZyme-focused metatranscriptomics using dbCAN annotation and taxonomic assignment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polymer breakdown releases fermentable intermediates that support downstream methane formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Elevated xylanase expression under thermophilic conditions supported enhanced early rice straw hydrolysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fermentation and methanogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rice straw amendment; incubation at 30 °C or 45 °C
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Polysaccharide-derived carbon flux supporting methane formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant to understanding residue decomposition and methane emission under warming.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional CAZyme class rather than a single identified gene locus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: At 45 °C peaked around day 16; more distributed over time at 30 °C
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal β-1,4-xylosidic linkages in xylan.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Alpha-galactosidase transcripts were among the key enzymes for rice straw degradation. 2. At 45 °C, dominant transcript contributions came from Firmicutes families including Clostridiaceae, Ruminococcaceae, and Paenibacillaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Releases sugars from plant polymers that fuel anaerobic food webs and methanogenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to enhanced thermophilic plant polymer deconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Polysaccharide-derived carbon flux supporting methane formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for understanding residue decomposition ecology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional CAZyme class rather than a single gene locus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected as key rice straw degradation activity; strongest thermophilic Firmicutes contribution
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes terminal alpha-galactosyl residues from oligo- and polysaccharides.

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Beta-galactosidase transcripts were listed among key enzymes involved in rice straw degradation. 2. At 45 °C, expression was mainly associated with Firmicutes families including Clostridiaceae, Ruminococcaceae, and Paenibacillaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides fermentable sugars for downstream acetate and methane formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to thermophilic enhancement of plant polymer decomposition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Polysaccharide-derived carbon flux supporting methane formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful ecological marker for residue decomposition under warming.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional CAZyme class rather than a single gene locus.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected as key rice straw degradation activity; notable thermophilic Firmicutes contribution
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes terminal beta-galactosyl residues from oligo- and polysaccharides.
